title: sedum oregonense
intro: Sedum oregonense, also known as Oregon stonecrop or Oregon sedum, is a succulent perennial plant native to the western United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it potentially useful in treating wounds and infections. The health benefits of Sedum oregonense include its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its ability to support the immune system. Sedum oregonense can be prepared for herbal use by drying the leaves and stems, then infusing them in hot water or oil to create a tea or topical application.
